# Practical Learning Techniques

**Technique 1: Use the Pomodoro. 25 minutes on, 5 minutes off.**

**Technique 2: Test Yourself. Cover the page. Recall what you read.**

**Technique 3: Use Anki. Spaced repetition app for long-term memory.**

**Case: The Hard Book. Survey chapter, turn headings into questions, read to answer, recall.**

**Case: The Math Problem. Study worked examples, then solve similar problems.**

**Case: The Language. Learn vocabulary with spaced repetition and active recall.**
---

*Generated by [Heardly App](https://www.heard.ly) — turning books into knowledge you can Listen and Execute.*

*Generated by [Heardly App](https://www.heard.ly) — turning books into knowledge you can Listen and Execute.*

**Case: The SQ3R. Survey, Question, Read, Recite, Review — a classic reading method.**
**Case: The Cornell Notes. A structured note-taking system for active learning.**
**Case: The Mind Map. Visual diagram connecting ideas. Helps see the big picture.**
**Case: The Self-Explanation. Explaining a concept to yourself as you learn it strengthens understanding.**
**Case: The Flashcard. Physical or digital flashcards for spaced repetition practice.**
**Case: The Chunking Practice. Break a complex skill into smaller pieces. Master each piece.**
**Case: The Visualization. Mentally rehearsing a skill activates the same brain regions as physical practice.**
**Case: The Teaching. Teaching others is one of the most effective ways to learn.**
**Case: The Variation. Varying your practice conditions makes learning more robust.**
